capabilities: Design reliability testing procedures by leveraging meta.protocol derived knowledge bases to ensure comprehensive evaluation and validation of system reliability.

procedure.action: Compose meta.protocol derived knowledge bases into the reliability testing framework by using domain-specific tooling to ensure all protocol specifications are adhered to during test execution by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.

Specify success criteria for reliability tests by setting thresholds for key metrics such as error_count < 1 and result_valid = true, ensuring these are measurable and aligned with system requirements.

Invoke logging of operation_duration_ms and result_code to an audit trail using a structured logging framework to facilitate traceability and post-test analysis following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.
Develop automated validation scripts that assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true for each test case, ensuring consistent and repeatable test outcomes.
Regularly review and update the testing procedures by analyzing audit logs and test results to refine criteria and improve reliability metrics over time.

summary: Establish a structured approach to design reliability testing procedures by integrating meta.protocol derived knowledge bases, focusing on measurable success criteria and validation steps.
title: Reliability Testing Procedure Design

failureConditions: Test procedures become outdated when meta.protocol updates are not integrated, causing incomplete reliability evaluations.
Inaccurate test results when success criteria are not clearly defined, leading to false positives in reliability assessments.
Loss of traceability and auditability when operation_duration_ms and result_code are not logged, causing difficulties in post-test analysis.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
error_count < 1 and result_valid = true for each test case, ensuring consistent and repeatable test outcomes
